howItWorks.intro: This off-grid battery calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Capacity = (Daily Usage × Autonomy Days) ÷ Depth of Discharge
Battery Cost = Capacity (kWh) × Cost per kWh
LiFePO4: 5000+ cycles at 80% DOD; Lead-acid: 1000 at 50% DOD
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How many batteries do I need for off-grid?
faq.answer: For a home using 30 kWh/day with 3 days autonomy at 80% DOD: you need about 112.5 kWh of battery capacity. In 48V LiFePO4, that's about 2,344 Ah — roughly 5-8 large battery modules.

faq.question: LiFePO4 vs lead-acid for off-grid?
faq.answer: LiFePO4 costs 2× more upfront but lasts 3-5× longer, has 80% DOD (vs 50% for lead-acid), weighs 1/3 as much, and needs no maintenance. Lifetime cost is usually lower with lithium.

faq.question: What is depth of discharge (DOD)?
faq.answer: DOD is how much of the battery's capacity you use. LiFePO4: 80-90% DOD safely. AGM: 50% max recommended. Flooded lead-acid: 50% max. Deeper discharge shortens battery life.

howWeCalculate.intro: This off-grid battery calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
